messages.content:
A parent returns home for the evening and uses different smart devices to help the children with homework, turn on the oven, run the robot vacuum cleaner, and turn on the home security system for the night.

What allows the parent to perform all of these actions easily?


enterprise platforms


blockchain


internet of things


massive MIMO


I don't know this yet.

The parent is able to perform all of these actions easily due to the internet of things (IoT). IoT refers to a network of interconnected devices and objects that can communicate and exchange data. In this scenario, different smart devices such as smart speakers, smartphones, and smart appliances are all interconnected through the IoT, allowing the parent to control them remotely and perform various tasks with ease.

Why does it take so long for each generation of wireless communication technology to become broadly available?


Devices that support a new generation are typically not available until years after the network is fully rolled out.


Network infrastructure for the previous generation must be shut down before the rollout of the next generation.


Network infrastructure and cell towers need to be upgraded to support next generation hardware.


Consumers are resistant to change and have little demand for greater bandwidth.


I don't know this yet.

Among the options provided, the most likely reason for the delay in the broad availability of each generation of wireless communication technology is that network infrastructure and cell towers need to be upgraded to support next-generation hardware. Before a new generation of wireless technology can be widely accessible, the existing infrastructure needs to be updated or replaced. This involves upgrading cell towers, implementing new protocols, and making other necessary changes to ensure compatibility with the new technology. These infrastructure upgrades take time and resources, which contribute to the delay in broad availability.

What is a limitation of 5G mmWave, despite its high speed?


shorter range


higher latency


larger antenna size


lower bandwidth


I don't know this yet.

The limitation of 5G mmWave, despite its high speed, is its shorter range. mmWave signals are more easily obstructed and have lower penetration capabilities compared to lower frequency signals. As a result, the range of mmWave signals is relatively limited, necessitating the deployment of more base stations and infrastructure to ensure coverage. This makes it challenging to achieve widespread coverage in rural or suburban areas, where a denser network of base stations is required to overcome range limitations.

Why are 5G mmWave signals more likely to be blocked by physical barriers such as walls, buildings, and trees?


Its signals are transmitted at higher frequencies.


Its signals are transmitted at lower frequencies.


Its signals transmit less information than older signals.


It is designed to work only within a single building.


I don't know this yet.

5G mmWave signals are more likely to be blocked by physical barriers such as walls, buildings, and trees because they are transmitted at higher frequencies. Higher-frequency signals have shorter wavelengths, which means they have more difficulty passing through obstacles compared to lower-frequency signals. The shorter wavelengths of mmWave signals make them more susceptible to attenuation and blockage by objects in the environment, resulting in reduced signal strength and coverage. This is why 5G mmWave requires a denser network of base stations to overcome these limitations and provide consistent coverage.

A construction company is looking to improve safety and efficiency at its sites.

What is an example of a solution that requires the use of edge computing and the Internet of Things (IoT)?


an automated system that manages worker schedules and equipment needs


a database that logs information about worker hours and assignments


networks that connect construction sites to share data on project work


wearable digital tags that workers can use to request help from supervisors


I don't know this yet.

An example of a solution that requires the use of edge computing and the Internet of Things (IoT) in this scenario would be wearable digital tags that workers can use to request help from supervisors. These wearable devices could be equipped with sensors to detect potential hazards or emergencies. When a worker feels threatened or requires assistance, they can trigger an alert through the wearable device. The data from these devices would be collected and processed locally at the edge (within the construction site) to quickly identify the worker's location and send alerts to supervisors or emergency response teams. This solution combines IoT devices (wearable digital tags) with edge computing to ensure real-time monitoring and prompt response to potential safety issues.

What is a reason for users and businesses to adopt 5G networks?


The 4G network is failing and no improvements have been added since it was rolled out ten years ago.


Mobile devices designed for 4G will not connect to 5G networks and will be obsolete within the next two years.


Significant speed improvements in network technology that will enable new use cases and applications.


The 5G infrastructure is less complex than previous generations, so the rollout will go much more quickly.


I don't know this yet.

A significant reason for users and businesses to adopt 5G networks is the significant speed improvements in network technology that will enable new use cases and applications. 5G networks have the capability to deliver faster download and upload speeds, lower latency, and higher capacity compared to previous generations like 4G. This speed improvement opens up possibilities for emerging technologies such as autonomous vehicles, virtual reality, augmented reality, and the Internet of Things. The enhanced speed and capacity of 5G networks enable faster data transfer, real-time communication, and support for bandwidth-intensive applications and services, making it an attractive choice for users and businesses seeking advanced connectivity.

Edge computing is an extension of which technology?


artificial intelligence


blockchain


cloud


quantum computing


I don't know this yet.

Edge computing is an extension of cloud computing. Edge computing involves processing data and performing computations at the edge of the network, closer to the data source or the end user, rather than solely relying on centralized cloud servers. It brings computational power closer to the devices or sensors generating the data, reducing latency and bandwidth requirements by processing data locally. While artificial intelligence, blockchain, and quantum computing can all be integrated with edge computing, the fundamental concept of edge computing is based on extending the capabilities of cloud computing infrastructure to the network edge.

A large offshore oil platform needs to transmit signals between different devices on the platform with high reliability and low latency. However, they are unable to cover the platform with their existing Wi-Fi connections.

What is the best solution for the platform in this scenario?


adding a satellite link to the existing Wi-Fi network


routing all their web traffic through the company headquarters


setting up a private 5G network on the platform itself


building new 5G cell sites between the platform and the shore


I don't know this yet.

The best solution for the offshore oil platform in this scenario would be to set up a private 5G network on the platform itself. Since the existing Wi-Fi connections do not provide sufficient coverage, implementing a private 5G network would allow for high reliability and low latency communication between different devices on the platform. 5G technology offers significant improvements in terms of bandwidth, speed, and capacity, which are crucial for supporting the communication needs of a large offshore platform. By deploying a private 5G network, the platform can ensure a dedicated and robust network infrastructure tailored to its specific requirements, without relying on external connections such as satellites or routing traffic through company headquarters.

What does the Internet of Things (IoT) enable?


It increases the transmission range of network signals.


It translates signals between different network generations.


It controls network traffic in highly populated areas.


It allows everyday physical devices to exchange data.


I don't know this yet.

The Internet of Things (IoT) enables everyday physical devices to exchange data. IoT refers to a network of interconnected devices, objects, and sensors that can communicate with each other and exchange information over the internet. These devices, often embedded with sensors or actuators, can collect and share data, enabling them to interact and make informed decisions. IoT technology enables seamless communication and data exchange between devices, leading to increased automation, enhanced monitoring and control, and improved efficiency in various domains such as smart homes, industrial automation, healthcare, and more.
